title: Master of Design (M.Des.) in Interior and Spatial Design
content: The M.Des. in Interior and Spatial Design prepares designers to shape human-centred, sustainable and culturally resonant interior environments—from single-room interventions to large, mixed-use adaptive-reuse projects. Students develop a synthesis of spatial thinking, material innovation, lighting, furniture systems, building-code literacy and digital workflows (BIM, parametric modelling, VR/AR). Live studios, community partnerships and industry collaborations anchor learning in real contexts, while research seminars cultivate critical perspectives on wellbeing, heritage, and environmental stewardship. What Students Will Learn Graduation in any discipline with minimum 50% marks Students with a UCEED / CUET score will be given preference in the merit list Interior Architect / Spatial Experience Designer / Adaptive-Reuse Specialist / Workplace & Facility Strategist / Smart-Interiors (IoT-VR) Consultant / Sustainable Interiors & Circular-Design Advisor / Design Researcher / Design Strategist / Project Manager in Interior & Architecture Firms / Academic or Industry Trainer 01 Design Knowledge – Apply interior-design theory, building science and digital tools to complex problems. 02 Research & Analysis – Investigate user behaviour, cultural context and environmental data to inform design. 03 Solution Development – Generate and iterate spatial concepts, furniture and lighting systems that integrate codes and sustainability. 04 Modern Tool Usage – Employ BIM, VR/AR, parametric and fabrication technologies. 05 Ethics & Codes – Interpret building regulations, universal-design and green-building standards. 06 Communication – Convey ideas through drawings, 3D models, visualisations and verbal presentations. 07 Teamwork & Leadership – Lead and collaborate in multidisciplinary environments. 08 Project Management & Entrepreneurship – Manage budgets, timelines and client relations; explore venture opportunities. 09 Impact & Sustainability – Evaluate social, cultural and ecological implications of interior interventions. 10 Lifelong Learning – Adapt to evolving materials, technologies and societal needs. 01 Innovative Spatial Practice – Create functional, aesthetic and sustainable interior environments. 02 Societal Impact – Address wellbeing, cultural heritage and environmental challenges through design. 03 Leadership & Collaboration – Excel in multidisciplinary project teams, upholding ethics and codes. 04 Lifelong Learning & Enterprise – Pursue research, licensure, entrepreneurship or doctoral studies. 01 Design healthy, ergonomic and emotionally engaging interior spaces. 02 Integrate advanced digital workflows (BIM/VR) for coordinated documentation and immersive stakeholder reviews. 03 Apply adaptive-reuse and circular-design principles to extend building life-cycles. 04 Produce evidence-based design strategies grounded in post-occupancy evaluation.
